warnings: The sheet groups 7 opening types across two rows (4 in row 1, 3 in row 2). Each item has its own circled/hex reference number printed on the sheet (1,2,3 then a second 1 in row 1; 2,3,4 in row 2) — the numbering visibly RESTARTS for windows vs doors (both a door and a window are labeled '1', '2', '3'), consistent with the sheet title covering two separate schedules (ประตู/หน้าต่าง) on one page. I re-labeled them D1-D3 / W1-W4 in extraction order (not the raw printed digit) to keep element_id unique within this file — the raw printed digit is preserved in the description above per item.

No explicit 'ประตู'/'หน้าต่าง' heading text was legible directly above each row to confirm the split with certainty; the door/window classification is inferred from hardware text (ลูกบิด present => door) and cross-checked against material consistency (aluminum+glass items grouped as windows). W2 in particular (row 2, item 1) is the most uncertain of the 7 and is flagged for zoom review.
This sheet was never extracted in mk_test/t1 or t2 (those rounds only covered pages 11/13/14 of this architectural section) — this is a first-time read.

Height breakdowns (e.g. 1.20+0.85) are read directly off the printed dimension sub-splits and are legible at this resolution; width breakdown for W3 is printed as 'EQ.'/'EQ.' (equal split) rather than a number, so no numeric width_breakdown could be extracted for it.
